steps.title: Link technique to style
steps.content: Demps imitated Hair's fast painting technique, which helped produce the impressionistic qualities now associated with the Florida Highwaymen's shared aesthetic.

steps.title: Compare Demps and McLendon
steps.content: McLendon painted with greater attention to detail, so his works are presented as less typical of that fast-painted group style. The passage uses McLendon as a contrast: he was prolific but did not fully embrace the fast, impressionistic approach. It therefore concerns typicality, not artistic superiority or originality. A and C add unsupported quality judgments, and B reverses the imitation claim. The answer is D.
